scaled_oracle_cases.description: Transformed seed case with larger model (3 reactions, 3 compounds), floating-point bounds, special characters in reaction names, and floating-point stoichiometric coefficients. Verifies correct variable definitions and exact bounds.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that the function correctly handles a larger metabolic model with floating-point bounds, special characters in reaction/compound names, and floating-point stoichiometric coefficients, producing correct variable definitions and mass balance constraints.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Variable bounds must match model limits exactly (including floating-point values), and keys in variable dictionaries must preserve special characters (spaces, underscores) as provided in the model.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Solutions that truncate bounds to integers, strip whitespace from names, or assume a fixed number of reactions/compounds may produce incorrect variable dictionaries or missing constraints.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
model = MetabolicModel()
solver = Solver('glpk')
prob, v, zi, dgri, xij, compounds = make_tmfa_problem(model, solver)
print("Namespaces:", list(prob.namespaces.keys()))
print("Flux vars:", v.vars)
print("Binary vars:", zi.vars)
print("Gibbs vars:", dgri.vars)
print("Concentration vars:", xij.vars)
print("Compounds:", compounds)
result = {'prob': prob, 'v': v, 'zi': zi, 'dgri': dgri, 'xij': xij, 'compounds': compounds}
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the behavior with larger model and floating-point coefficients.
Scaled executable cases must capture the floating-point bounds in the output.
Scaled executable cases must verify the exact keys in variable dictionaries.
